headline: 3 Dividend Stocks That Pay More Than Social Security COLA
summary: Social Security's 2026 cost-of-living adjustment barely keeps pace with a grocery bill, but three dividend stocks have been outpacing it for decades with yields that range from nearly 4% to almost 7%.

raw.headline: 3 Dividend Stocks That Pay More Than Social Security COLA

headline: A $1.4 Million Portfolio That Delivers Reliable Income Through Bull and Bear Markets
summary: A $1.4 million nest egg is far above the typical U.S. retirement account balance, but the check it writes each month depends entirely on how the assets are arranged. At a 3.5% yield, the portfolio produces $49,000 a year. At a 10% yield, it produces $140,000. The difference looks simple on a spreadsheet, but it ... A $1.4 Million Portfolio That Delivers Reliable Income Through Bull and Bear Markets

headline: Meet Wall Street’s Safest High-Yield Dividend Stocks
summary: Most high-yield dividend stocks are traps disguised as income, but a handful on the NYSE have survived recessions, rate shocks, and earnings disasters without missing a single payment. Three of them are hiding in plain sight right now.

raw.headline: Meet Wall Street’s Safest High-Yield Dividend Stocks
raw.summary: Most high-yield dividend stocks are traps disguised as income, but a handful on the NYSE have survived recessions, rate shocks, and earnings disasters without missing a single payment. Three of them are hiding in plain sight right now.

headline: A Dividend Portfolio That Can Cover the Cost of Living in San Francisco
summary: San Francisco makes passive income math unforgiving. SmartAsset’s 2026 comfort-salary study estimates that a single adult needs about $134,950 in pretax income to live comfortably in the city, among the highest figures in the country. Turning that paycheck into dividend income is not just a yield exercise. The yield an investor reaches for changes both ... A Dividend Portfolio That Can Cover the Cost of Living in San Francisco

headline: Realty Income Boosts Liquidity: What Does This Mean for Growth?
summary: O expands liquidity with $5.5B revolving credit facilities and boosts commercial paper capacity to $5.5B from $3B, supporting global growth.

headline: The Real Risk in Retirement Isn’t Running Out of Money. It’s Losing Your Purchasing Power.
summary: Retirement planning fixates on depletion risk. The quieter problem is that a portfolio can hold its dollar value for thirty years and still leave a retiree poorer in real terms. The CPI-U rose from 308.417 in January 2024 to 335.123 in May 2026, while the 2026 Social Security COLA was 2.8%. Core PCE inflation reached ... The Real Risk in Retirement Isn’t Running Out of Money. It’s Losing Your Purchasing Power.

headline: What It Takes to Age in Place, And the Portfolio That Pays For It
summary: The house is paid off. The kids have moved out. Yet the number that may determine whether you can stay there for the next 25 years is not the home’s value or the old mortgage balance. It is the price of the services that keep the house livable when driving, cooking, cleaning, and climbing stairs ... What It Takes to Age in Place, And the Portfolio That Pays For It
